items.name: Ardent
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Ardent (YC P26) – Postgres sandboxes in seconds with zero migration
items.summary: Postgres sandboxes for coding agents, with fast branch clones and anonymization.
items.why_included: Launch HN thread with strong comment support for safer DB testing.
items.evidence: Commenters said it works on restricted hosted Postgres and praised branch hooks plus obfuscated replicas.

items.name: Xata
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Ardent (YC P26) – Postgres sandboxes in seconds with zero migration
items.summary: Postgres branching platform with copy-on-write clones and anonymized data.
items.why_included: Recommended as the existing Neon-like alternative in Ardent comments.
items.evidence: One commenter explicitly said to 'have a look at Xata' for a Neon/git-like Postgres experience.

items.name: Optery
items.source_story_title: Setting up a free *.city.state.us locality domain (2025)
items.summary: Data-broker removal service that scrubs personal information from public sites.
items.why_included: A commenter gave firsthand praise for its long-tail privacy cleanup.
items.evidence: The thread said they used Optery themselves and still found themselves hard to locate afterward.

items.name: Xs of Y
items.source_story_title: Xs of Y – roguelike that names itself every run. Written in 4kLoC
items.summary: A 4kLoC roguelike that renames itself every run.
items.why_included: The comments were unusually enthusiastic for a tiny indie game.
items.evidence: Replies called it awesome, loved the dwarf-fortress vibes, and asked for the repo star.

items.name: Rars
items.source_story_title: Rars: a Rust RAR implementation, mostly written by LLMs
items.summary: Rust RAR implementation, mostly written with LLM assistance.
items.why_included: Novel tooling project with a surprisingly positive HN response.
items.evidence: Commenters called it a really cool project, starred the repo, and discussed the compression workflow.
